channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nationals prepare for leadership ballot following Littleproud’s resignation
item.description: David Littleproud has unexpectedly stepped down as leader of the Nationals, catching both his party and Coalition colleagues off guard. The resignation was announced after a courtesy call to Prime Min...

item.title: CBI schedules further questioning of TVK chief Vijay in Karur stampede case
item.description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has issued a fresh summons to actor and Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) president, Vijay, for further questioning in connection with the Karur stampede case, n...

item.title: Motorists Ask NTSA For Details On Instant Traffic Fines Process
item.description: A Kenyan motorists' lobby, the Motorists Association of Kenya (MAK), is pushing the National Transport and Safety Authority (NTSA) to provide more clarity on the newly launched instant traffic fines s...

item.title: Tanggol Expresses Deep Gratitude to Ponggay in Episode 796 of Batang Quiapo
item.description: In the latest episode of 'Batang Quiapo', Episode 796, the character Tanggol expresses heartfelt gratitude for the sacrifices made by Ponggay. The episode delves into the emotional bond between the tw...

item.title: Akwa Ibom Community Demands Official Recognition For Legit Village Head
item.description: Some people wey dey Mbiabong Ikpe Village, for Ikpe Annang Clan, Essien Udim Local Government Area, Akwa Ibom State, don come together dey ask state government make dem officially recognize Chief Samu...

item.title: Penetanguishene Resident Rescued During Weekend Ice Rescue Operation
item.description: A man from Penetanguishene was among several individuals rescued over the weekend after becoming stranded on ice. Emergency services responded swiftly to the dangerous situation, ensuring that all inv...

item.title: NEMA Calls for Public Input on Draft E-Waste and Environmental Regulations
item.description: The National Environment Management Authority (NEMA) in Kenya has issued a public call for participation in a national validation meeting aimed at finalising key environmental regulations. The meeting...

item.title: How Trust Networks and Brokers Drive Cryptocurrency Adoption in Nigeria's Gadget Imports from China
item.description: For Naija, crypto no be just about fancy blockchain or Bitcoin hype. E dey really help traders for Lagos Computer Village do quick business with Chinese suppliers. Instead of queuing for bank forex we...

item.title: Fourth Accelerate Women’s Executive Summit to Empower Tanzanian Women in Leadership
item.description: Dar es Salaam is set to host the fourth Accelerate Women’s Executive Summit on Saturday, 14th March at Johari Rotana Hotel. The one-day event is organised by the Accelerate Foundation and aims to brin...

item.title: PDP Makinde Faction Convenes Emergency BoT Meeting in Abuja Amid Party Crisis
item.description: The faction of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) loyal to Oyo State Governor Seyi Makinde held an emergency expanded meeting of its Board of Trustees (BoT) on Tuesday, March 10, 2026, in Asokoro, Abu...

item.title: How the Iran Conflict is Affecting UK Finances and Energy Costs
item.description: The conflict between the US-Israel coalition and Iran is already affecting global and UK finances, particularly through rising energy costs. Oil prices spiked following airstrikes on Tehran, causing U...

item.title: Pakistan Receives Multiple Fuel Shipments to Maintain Supply Amid Global Shortages
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan has received four ships carrying essential fuel, including petrol and diesel, to help manage domestic shortages caused by global supply disruptions. One ship, Torm Damini, already ...

item.title: African Militaries Shift Towards Local Production to Reduce Reliance on Foreign Weapons
item.description: African armies have historically depended heavily on foreign powers like China, Russia, the United States, Turkey, and France for weapons and military technology. This reliance often leads to serious ...

item.title: Inmate Charged Following Death of Ian Huntley at HMP Frankland
item.description: A prison inmate, Anthony Russell, aged 43, has been formally charged with the murder of Ian Huntley, the convicted Soham murderer. Russell is due to appear via video link at Newton Aycliffe Magistrate...

item.title: Forbes 2026 Billionaires List: Record 3,428 Billionaires Worth $20.1 Trillion, Elon Musk Tops with $839 Billion
item.description: Forbes released its 40th annual World's Billionaires List on March 10, 2026, revealing a record 3,428 billionaires worldwide, up from 3,028 the previous year. Their combined wealth reached an all-time...

item.title: Exiled Qatari Opposition Leader Advocates for Political Reform and Peace with Israel
item.description: Sheikh Khalid Al-Hail, a former insider of Qatar’s ruling elite and now exiled in the U.K., is actively working to establish a government-in-waiting for Qatar. Al-Hail, president of the Qatari Nationa...

item.title: Encouraging Tablet Use in Children Could Improve Treatment and Reduce Costs
item.description: Many children are prescribed liquid medicines, which are often assumed to be the safest and most practical option. However, giving liquids can be challenging: children may spit them out, swallow only ...

item.title: Pakistan Denies Sending Delegation to Kabul Amid Tensions with Afghan Taliban
item.description: On 10th March 2026, Pakistani official sources dismissed reports circulating on social media and cited by Afghan media that Pakistan had sent a three-member delegation to Kabul to hold talks with the ...

item.title: MaXhosa Africa showcases South African culture at Paris Fashion Week with custard-inspired runway
item.description: MaXhosa Africa stunned audiences at Paris Fashion Week with a unique ready-to-wear runway show that celebrated South African culture in a highly theatrical way. The highlight of the show was a playful...

item.title: AFP maintains West Philippine Sea patrols amid national fuel-saving measures
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) announced that military operations in the West Philippine Sea will proceed as planned even with the ongoing global oil crisis and government directives to cut...
